rulings.Case Title: RICHARD A DENTON VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Revised Renewed Motion And Revised Renewed Motion To Reconsider The Order Voiding The Notice Of Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 21, 2019, Line 1, PLAINTIFF RICHARD DENTON's Revised Renewed Motion And Revised Renewed Motion To Reconsider The Order Voiding The Notice Of Settlement Plaintiff Richard Denton's "revised renewed motion to reconsider" is denied. Even assuming plaintiff's characterization of the appellate record, I cannot, for several reasons, grant the relief sought - enforcement of what plaintiff calls the "settlement agreement." First, a settlement would require approval by the Board of Supervisors and the Public Utilities Commission, but the record is bare of evidence that either approval occurred. Second, the settlement cannot be enforced under CCP §664.6 because that statute requires direct party participation. The parties must sign a written stipulation for entry of judgment or the parties must enter into a stipulation "orally before the court." (CCP § 664.6; Levy v. Sup. Ct. (1995) 10 Cal.4th 578, 584.) Neither occurred here. Third, plaintiff seeks a settlement that would only resolve the case through September 18, 2015, and would not dispose of the entire case. But the City has not agreed to a piecemeal settlement. Fourth, plaintiff seeks all his attorney fees to enforce the settlement, litigate the appeal, etc., but "[c]onsent is not mutual, unless the parties all agree upon the same thing in the same sense." (Civ. Code §1580.) The City never agreed to pay the attorney fees plaintiff seeks. Fifth, there is no evidence the purported settlement was ratified; plaintiff fails to show that the City received an advantage from it. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portions of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/rbu

rulings.Case Title: EVA ARMADA ET AL VS. MICHAEL GUMBAN PANGILINAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of And Motion To Compel Discovery Responses To Be Verified
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 21, 2019, Line 1, PLAINTIFFs EVA ARMADA, ANTONIO SALSEDO, RUDY CONCEPCION, and LORNA BINALEY's Motion To Compel Discovery Responses To Be Verified Pro Tem Judge Julia Campins, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Although the verifications are extremely tardy and counsel for Defendants should have updated counsel for Plaintiffs more quickly in response to the meet and confer letter, given that the verifications have now been provided, the Motion is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to julia@campinsbenhambaker.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ALBERT LUJAN VS. DAVID SMALL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Second Amended And Supplemental Comlaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 21, 2019, Line 8, PLAINTIFF ALBERT LUJAN's Motion And Motion For Leave To File Second Amended And Supplemental Complaint Plaintiff Albert Lujan's motion for leave to file a second amended and supplemental complaint is GRANTED. California courts liberally allow amendments to pleadings absent a showing of prejudice. (Douglas v. Sup. Ct. (1989) 215 Cal.App.3d 155, 158; CCP §576.) If the amended complaint is believed defective, CCP §430.41 is to be followed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/rbu
